mentions.note: Never really declined; Cramer thinks stock ready to roll higher given market recovery and still-solid fundamentals.
Rebounded after Jassy's strategic letter clarified AWS slowdown narrative; satellite acquisition added momentum. Weakness on retail, slowing AWS, and CEO comparison to Bezos fears unwarranted.
Trading 27x earnings with real Google competitive threat in app-marketing space. Let bounce to 450-460 then trim; don't chase here.
Project management platform facing material AI displacement risk. Cramer agrees with selling pressure; expects AI automation to hurt fundamentals.

Online travel with Priceline, OpenTable brands down 22% on AI disruption fears. Trades 17x forward earnings with 17.6% growth; Europe squeeze priced in, confident Q1 guidance. People will vacation when war ends.
Has high-quality private credit holdings. CEO Larry Fink told Cramer oil could be cut in half when war ends, signaling confidence in market outlook; bullish on solar as growth driver.
Cloud-based insurance software; Cramer prefers Berkshire Hathaway for insurance exposure or Lemonade if seeking AI-embedded InsurTech alternative.
Caller bought at 39, down to 28; Europe tough. Will come back but Cramer suggests switching to Banco Santander for better European exposure when bounces.

Solar panel maker down 30% from Dec 2025 peak; 27% earnings growth at 11x PE. Trump bias against wind not solar; high tariffs favor domestic production. BlackRock CEO Fink bullish on solar as growth driver.
Young, profitable, debt-free B2B e-commerce company; Cramer met founder and impressed. Stock keeps climbing on inexpensive valuation.
Dinged last year but recovered all losses. Narrative about eating Nvidia's lunch overstated; company's AI chips still maturing.
AI-embedded InsurTech alternative to CCC; Cramer prefers this over cloud-based insurance software if seeking that exposure.
Elliott activist turnaround; down 25% trading below 15x earnings despite 3x EPS growth. Oil estimates already cut from $4 to $2.86 per share. Potential M&A target if industry consolidates.
Surged 4.4% on announcement of new AI model Llama; talent spending finally paying off. Earlier fears about wasted AI investment dissipated.
Behind on AI; could be hurt by Anthropic and OpenAI competition. Business growth not as strong as hoped, but balance sheet strength allows for strategic pivots if management acts.
Recovered to 196 from 165 lows; Google/Amazon lunch-eating fears and China order worries never materialized. Overvaluation narrative proved wrong; now reasonably valued on forward earnings.
Caller's Deutsche Bank down from 39 to 28; Cramer recommends switching to Santander under CEO Ana Botín for European exposure on path to breakout. Better European play than DB.
Down 29% from Sept 2025 high; 40% EPS growth, robo-taxi fears overblown via Nuro partnership. 200M monthly users create network moat. Cheap for first time in years at 21.3x earnings.

Premium cruise operator leading category five consecutive years; quality obsession, contrarian expansion into Egypt/Great Lakes, fuel hedged at only 5% of revenue. Stock up 10% post-earnings despite consumer headwinds.
Independent power producer; EPS doubling in 2026 on nuclear + data center demand. Down 25%, trades 19x earnings—bargain growth. Overextended rally last fall warranted recent pullback.
Surprised by large private credit exposure; worst of major banks on that front. Missed a quarter but stock trying to bottom; Cramer owns it for trust on valuation despite disappointment.
